findings.title: server.ts onCancel marks store blocked but never kills running pipeline or resolves approval gate
findings.detail: The public control-plane on port 3001 handles cancel by calling store.updateState(taskId, 'blocked', ...) only. The daemon's port-3002 handler also aborts the AbortController and resolves any pending HITL approval gate. A cancel routed to port 3001 leaves the pipeline worker running indefinitely, burning tokens and holding the worktree, while the task appears cancelled in the UI.
findings.fix_sketch: Document the partial-action via console.error so the operator knows the cancel was not fully actioned. Route full cancellation through port 3002 (the daemon) for AbortController + ApprovalGate wiring.

findings.title: circuit-breaker recordOutcome re-enables disabled arms on a single bare success, bypassing probe gate
findings.detail: The success branch in recordOutcome transitions both 'probing' and 'disabled' arms to 'active'. A disabled arm (in cooldown after hitting the failure threshold) should only be re-enabled via a probe; a stray successful task that runs against a still-cooling arm bypasses the probing mechanism entirely, re-enabling a repeatedly failing model without the intended probe gate.
findings.fix_sketch: Change the success branch to only transition when state.status === 'probing'. A bare disabled success should reset consecutiveFailures to 0 but leave the arm disabled until onAssignment drains the cooldown.

findings.title: runner.ts logCosts uses task.id as sprintId fallback, corrupting sprint-level cost aggregation
findings.detail: In DefaultPipelineRunner.run(), logCosts falls back to input.task.id when input.sprintId is undefined. summarizeCosts() groups cost records by sprintId; using the task ID as the group key means each task appears as its own sprint in cost reports rather than rolling up into the parent sprint.
findings.fix_sketch: Require sprintId in PipelineInput or derive it at call sites. If legitimately absent, use a sentinel like 'unaffiliated' and document that cost rollup is unavailable for those tasks.

findings.title: defaultHaikuCall passes full prompt (including user brief) as argv — visible in ps aux
findings.detail: In auto-router.ts, the defaultHaikuCall passes the entire router prompt via execFile(['--print', prompt, ...]) as a positional CLI argument. The prompt includes quoteAsUserData(p.body) which wraps the full issue brief. The process argument list is visible to any local user with ps aux access, leaking task content.
findings.fix_sketch: Switch to stdin delivery by writing the prompt to stdin or a temp file and using the --file option if supported. The comment about stdin unreliability should be addressed by flushing the write explicitly.

findings.title: control-plane.ts parseCommand places no length cap on reason, userId, userLabel, or idempotencyKey
findings.detail: Only the goal field is capped at 10,000 characters. Fields like reason (cancel), userLabel, userId, and idempotencyKey are accepted at arbitrary length. A crafted request could place a 60KB string in userLabel and have it stored verbatim in the SQLite tasks table, Discord outbox, and log lines.
findings.fix_sketch: Add MAX_FIELD_LEN (e.g. 512) caps to reason, userId, userLabel, idempotencyKey, and messageId. Truncate or reject on excess, consistent with the goal field's existing validation.

findings.title: issue-commenter.ts lastCommentId mutable closure races if comment() called before waitForApproval resolves
findings.detail: createIssueCommenter() uses a shared mutable lastCommentId closure variable. If comment() is called to post a progress update while waitForApproval is mid-poll on a prior plan comment, lastCommentId shifts to the new comment ID and the approval poll watches the wrong comment.
findings.fix_sketch: Capture the comment ID in waitForApproval's local scope at the start of the call (const commentId = lastCommentId) rather than referencing the closure variable throughout the poll.

findings.title: queue/github.ts findLabelAddedAt paginates all issue events with no page count cap
findings.detail: findLabelAddedAt uses octokit.paginate() without a maxPages or perPage limit beyond 100 per request. On an issue with many events this can fetch thousands of events across many API pages, burning rate-limit budget and adding latency to every sweepCooldowns tick.
findings.fix_sketch: Add an explicit page limit of 5-10 pages to the paginate call. Alternatively, use the most-recent-first events endpoint since the latest label application is what matters for cooldown calculation.

findings.title: DockerSandboxRunner falls back to in-worktree execution without requiring IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK
findings.detail: When Docker is unreachable and NODE_ENV is not 'production', runInWorktree sets useFallback=true and runs pnpm directly in the worktree with no isolation. Any malicious or buggy test script runs with full filesystem and network access. The banner emitted is informational only; result status is still 'passed' if pnpm exits 0.
findings.fix_sketch: Require explicit opt-in via IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK for non-production environments too, or at minimum emit a WARN-level structured log and return status 'partial' instead of 'passed' when running in fallback mode.

findings.title: runner.ts logCosts only called on pr_opened path — failed/cancelled runs never record costs
findings.detail: In DefaultPipelineRunner.run(), await logCosts(input, attempts) appears only before the final return on the pr_opened branch. All other early returns (failed, cancelled, blocked_by_reviewer) skip cost logging entirely. Architect + plan-reviewer + doctor retries that ultimately fail are invisible to the cost ledger.
findings.fix_sketch: Move logCosts into a try/finally block wrapping the entire run() body, or call it just before each non-pr_opened return.

findings.title: audit-store.ts TERMINAL_SQL_LIST string interpolation is structurally fragile
findings.detail: TERMINAL_SQL_LIST is built by mapping over TERMINAL_AUDIT_STATUSES and wrapping each value in single quotes, then interpolating directly into SQL strings. The values are static enum members today, but a future enum value containing a single quote would silently produce malformed SQL.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace the IN-list approach with positional parameters using = ANY($N::text[]) or generate numbered parameters from the enum length. Add a unit test that verifies each member matches the expected safe-character set.

findings.title: queue/store.ts recoverStale reads IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS env on every call rather than once at construction
findings.detail: TaskStore.recoverStale() calls process.env['IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS'] on every invocation. A live change to the env variable between calls changes the effective max attempts mid-run, which can cause a task to flip between recovery and failure branches across sweep cycles.
findings.fix_sketch: Read IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS once in the TaskStore constructor and store it as a private field. Document that it requires a process restart to take effect.

findings.title: workers/claude.ts passes full brief as -p argv — visible in ps aux for all pipeline phases
findings.detail: buildClaudeArgs() builds args starting with ['-p', wrapped, ...] where wrapped includes the full issue body. All pipeline phases (architect, editor, doctor) pass their prompts as argv positionals to the claude subprocess, making them visible to any user with /proc access on the host.
findings.fix_sketch: Pass the brief via stdin using the spawn approach instead of -p. Alternatively, write the brief to a temp file and use --file <path> if the claude CLI supports it, cleaning up after spawn.

findings.title: VerifierAgent defaults to StubSandboxRunner — all runs pass unconditionally if sandbox not injected
findings.detail: VerifierAgent in src/agents/verifier/index.ts defaults opts.sandbox to new StubSandboxRunner() which returns status='passed' unconditionally. If wiring code omits the DockerSandboxRunner injection, every PR passes verification without running any checks. The daemon correctly uses VerifierController (defaults to DockerSandboxRunner), but VerifierAgent remains a silent trap.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a startup warning in the VerifierAgent constructor when opts.sandbox is absent. Longer term, require explicit injection and provide a named STUB_SANDBOX constant for tests.

findings.title: pipeline/reviewer.ts is a deprecated re-export shim with no enforced removal deadline
findings.detail: The file contains only a re-export from ./diff-reviewer.js with a comment 'Remove this file after the next release cuts.' No issue or ADR tracks the removal. With automated agents shipping PRs continuously, the shim may persist indefinitely.
findings.fix_sketch: Open a GitHub issue labeled 'chore' to delete the file, update all importers to reference './diff-reviewer.js' directly, and merge in a single PR.

findings.title: spawn-runner.ts cancel() does not short-circuit the SIGTERM grace period if process already exited
findings.detail: The cancel() implementation sends SIGTERM then waits 5000ms before sending SIGKILL, without checking whether the process already exited during the grace period. If the process exits immediately, the 5-second setTimeout still runs and may attempt SIGKILL on an already-gone pid.
findings.fix_sketch: Clear the SIGKILL timer in the child's 'close' handler so it is cancelled when the process exits cleanly after SIGTERM.

findings.title: auto-router in-memory cache never expires — stale routing decisions persist across sprint submissions
findings.detail: The cache in auto-router.ts is a module-level Map keyed by SHA-256 of title+body+labels. It is never evicted within a process lifetime. If the same issue body is re-submitted after SPRINT.md changes or learnings are updated, the cached routing decision from the first submission is returned.
findings.fix_sketch: Include a TTL per cache entry or hash the learnings file content into the cache key. At minimum, document that the cache is deliberately LRU-free and process restart is the eviction mechanism.

findings.title: contracts/hmac.ts verifyPayload has redundant length check after timingSafeEqual
findings.detail: After padding mismatched-length buffers with zeros and running timingSafeEqual, the code also checks raw.length === expected.length. Since a zero-padded buffer can never produce a valid signature, the extra check is redundant with the padding logic above it.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the trailing && raw.length === expected.length clause and add a comment explaining why length mismatch is implicitly rejected by the constant-time compare.

findings.title: server.ts deferringDiscordOut.postTaskCreated always returns empty threadId string without warning
findings.detail: deferringDiscordOut() returns { threadId: '' } from postTaskCreated without any log warning. Downstream code that checks for a non-empty threadId will silently skip thread updates for tasks ingested via the public port, and the empty string may be stored as the Discord source threadId.
findings.fix_sketch: Return null or undefined instead of '' for threadId when intentionally absent, and update the DiscordOut interface to reflect the nullable contract.

findings.title: audit-store.ts dbReadIndex returns null for both DB unreachable and zero-row cases without distinguishing them
findings.detail: dbReadIndex returns null when the DB is unreachable (catches a thrown error) and also when findings.length === 0. Callers cannot distinguish a connectivity failure (should alert) from a genuinely empty repo (normal fallback).
findings.fix_sketch: Return a discriminated result { found: false, reason: 'db_error' | 'empty' } instead of null so callers can emit a warning on db_error and silently fall back on empty.

findings.title: invariants.ts runCommand() accumulates output without byte cap — OOM risk on large semgrep scans
findings.detail: InvariantRunner.runCommand() pushes all stdout/stderr chunks into an unbounded array with no size limit. sandbox.ts has a matching runCommand() that caps at 1MB and appends '[output truncated]' — invariants.ts must mirror that guard to prevent daemon OOM on pathological scans.
findings.fix_sketch: Add totalBytes counter and cap = 1_000_000 sentinel matching sandbox.ts lines 419-432.

findings.title: scorer.ts maybeEmbedSprint() uses void cfg; dead expression instead of _cfg convention
findings.detail: The cfg parameter in maybeEmbedSprint() is unused. The code uses a void cfg; no-op statement rather than the idiomatic TypeScript _cfg prefix convention.
findings.fix_sketch: Rename parameter cfg: ProposerConfig to _cfg: ProposerConfig and remove the void cfg; statement.

findings.title: budget.ts enforceBudget declared async but contains no await — misleading signature
findings.detail: enforceBudget() is declared async but has a fully synchronous body. Removing async requires updating the ProposerStages interface in index.ts; deferred to avoid cross-file cascade.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove async from enforceBudget, change return type to DedupedCandidate[], update the interface in index.ts. All callers await it so behaviour is unchanged.
